channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Our Competitor Had an AI That Covered 97.2%. We Had a Spreadsheet and a Fake Quote. Guess Who Won.
item.description: You walk into the RFP briefing. Your competitor has 200 people, 97% AI coverage, and a 4-day delivery promise. You have 15 people and a proposal you haven't even finished writing. Do you bet on...

item.title: Perl PAGI Project Updates
item.description: Quick update to anyone interested in upcoming changes to the PAGI project (spiritual successor to Plack/PSGI). 1) Distribution split up: when we released PAGI, we initially released everything as...

item.title: Agent Framework RAG for Agents: Giving Your Agent the Right Context
item.description: This is Part 13 of my series on the Microsoft Agent Framework. You can read the original post over on lukaswalter.dev. In the previous article, we looked at workflows. Workflows make sense when...

item.title: No more lightbulbs, much more sports: Five predictions for Roku’s future
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. When Fox announced its...

item.title: Preparing Specs for AI Coding Agents
item.description: AI coding agents now edit repositories, run commands, and produce branches. That makes the spec before the work more important: it carries the context, boundaries, and success criteria the agent...

item.title: How to Integrate Apache Kafka with Spring Boot: A Production-Ready Guide
item.description: When a Spring Boot service needs to talk to another service without waiting on a synchronous HTTP call, message queues are the usual answer. Apache Kafka has become the default choice for this in...

item.title: I built Proofline because AI agents are getting too good at sounding finished
item.description: AI agents are getting very good at writing final reports. The problem is not only that they make mistakes. The problem is that sometimes they make mistakes with excellent presentation. Proofline...

item.title: General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at around $2B valuation
item.description: General Intuition is in talks to raise around $300 million at a roughly $2 billion valuation from backers including Jeff Bezos. The startup trains AI agents on spatial-temporal reasoning.

item.title: Gas Optimization That Doesn't Break Security: Storage, Calldata, and the Traps
item.description: Gas optimization is satisfying. You shave a few thousand gas off a function and feel clever. But some optimizations trade away safety in ways that are not obvious, and I have seen "optimized"...

item.title: Building a browser diagram editor: which import/export formats actually matter?
item.description: Disclosure up front: I'm affiliated with diagram.now — I'm connected to the product. I'm posting this to get developer feedback on diagram import/export interoperability, not to pitch an install....

item.title: The Road Toward Mainnet: A Security-First Approach to XRPL Lending Protocol
item.description: Over the last several months, XRP Ledger (XRPL) has fundamentally shifted in how amendments move from concept to mainnet. Historically, amendment development was largely focused on functional...

item.title: TerraPower in Deal with Meta for Eight Natrium 345 MW Advanced Nuclear Plants
item.description: Article URL: https://neutronbytes.com/2026/01/09/terrapower-in-mega-deal-with-meta-for-eight-natrium-345-mw-advanced-nuclear-plants/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48586648...

item.title: A tech worker-backed PAC is bringing a $5M knife to Big Tech’s $100M gunfight
item.description: Guardrails positions itself as a populist political movement that runs on small donations from people in the trenches of the AI boom.

item.title: Google Calendar finally has more color options for events
item.description: Running out of color options for events in Google Calendar shouldn't be an issue going forward. The previous limit of 11 predefined colors has now been expanded to give users access to up to 200...

item.title: Microsoft Details Windows Clipper Malware Campaign Using USB LNK Worm and Tor-Based C2
item.description: Microsoft has disclosed details of a Windows-based cryptocurrency clipper campaign that has targeted users since February 2026. "The clipper in this campaign relies on Windows Script Host and...

item.title: The midterms are going to be a data security nightmare
item.description: One messy database is threatening to disenfranchise thousands or even millions of registered voters, while leaving even more at risk of intimidation or data breaches, in the name of solving a...

item.title: The Verge’s guide to Amazon Prime Day 2026
item.description: Amazon Prime Day 2026 lifts off on June 23rd and will hopefully deliver the best deals of the summer. We’ve been covering the most notable pre-Prime Day discounts happening, and come next week,...

item.title: INC Ransomware Emerges as Major RaaS Threat in 2026 with 830+ Victims Since 2023
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have charted the evolution of INC from an nascent ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation to one of the most prolific cybercrime groups in 2026, claiming no less than...

item.title: The best robot vacuum deals available ahead of Prime Day
item.description: If you’ve been wanting to buy a robot vacuum but have been put off by how much it can cost to get a good one, now is not a bad time to start looking. Prime Day is next week, but many retailers are...

item.title: Who decides when AI is too dangerous?
item.description: On today’s episode of Decoder, my guest is Hayden Field, senior AI reporter for The Verge. Often when Hayden comes on the show, it’s because something has gone wrong in the world of AI. Last...

item.title: The Scripts on Your Checkout Page Are Now a PCI DSS Problem
item.description: An independent PCI assessor tested Reflectiz against the new PCI DSS rules. Here is the verdict: See the full QSA assessment here → When a customer types their card number into your checkout,...

item.title: DragonForce Hackers Abuse Microsoft Teams Relays to Hide Backdoor.Turn C2 Traffic
item.description: Threat actors associated with the DragonForce ransomware have been observed using a custom Go-based remote access trojan (RAT) called Backdoor.Turn to conceal command-and-control (C2) traffic...

item.title: Amazon’s Kindle Colorsoft bundle is almost half off for Prime Day
item.description: Amazon’s Kindle Colorsoft Essentials Bundle is on sale for $182.97 (originally $334.97) as an early Prime Day deal, the lowest price we’ve seen for the combo. Unlike other Kindles, the Colorsoft’s...

item.title: Photoshop and Premiere now have AI assistants
item.description: Adobe's plan to stick AI assistants into all of its Creative Cloud suite is now fully underway, with new chatbots now rolling out to its biggest editing and design apps. As part of a public beta...

item.title: Adobe’s redesigned AI studio remembers what your creations look like
item.description: Adobe is introducing some new capabilities for its Firefly AI assistant, alongside a "reimagined" AI studio that lets you edit and generate new designs from a single interface. The new Firefly...

item.title: The Download: a new hunt for dark matter and Kenya’s case for going solar
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The search for dark matter has been blown wide open For...

item.title: Pixi’s new iOS app turns text messages into interactive AR experiences
item.description: Forget stickers, GIFs, and emoji reactions. Pixi is betting that the next evolution of messaging is interactive augmented reality (AR).

item.title: This Ghost in the Shell keyboard makes me want to activate the hundred spidery robot fingers inside my regular fingers
item.description: Anime collaborations are everywhere, from Gundam watches and Naruto nights at Major League ballparks to just about anything Evangelion. But these Ghost in the Shell keyboards from Iqunix are some...

item.title: Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network
item.description: If an autonomous AI agent interacts with your company's core intellectual property today, can your security team instantly name the person who authorized it? For most enterprises, the answer is a...

item.title: Embedding Forbidden Text in Spyware to Discourage AI Analysis
item.description: At least one malware developer is adding text about nuclear and biological weapons to their spyware, in an effort to stop automatic AI analysis. Details: The _index.js payload begins with a large...
